title: Central-Berlin monthly meteorology (temperature, wind speed + direction components, precipitation) for the Umweltzone controlled-ITS, 2008-01 to 2011-12
publisher: Open-Meteo (ERA5 reanalysis, Copernicus Climate Change Service)
licence: CC BY 4.0 (Open-Meteo); contains modified Copernicus Climate Change Service information (ERA5)
vintage: Open-Meteo historical-weather (ERA5) archive for a central-Berlin point (52.52, 13.405), daily values aggregated to monthly means: temperature_2m_mean (C), wind_speed_10m_mean (km/h), direction-resolved wind components u/v (km/h), precipitation_sum (mm). One row per month over 2008-01..2011-12.
notes: Meteorology is the dominant short-window NO2 confounder. In the controlled ITS the in-zone urban-background control series already absorbs most shared dispersion, so this join enters as the meteorology-adjusted specification arm of the model average. Wind is stored as u/v components so a monthly mean respects direction.
